game_info.title: 서울 2033: 후원자 (Seoul 2033: Supporter)
game_info.concept: **서울 2033 : 후원자 (Seoul 2033: Supporter)** Seoul 2033 is a text-based roguelike where players navigate a post-apocalyptic Seoul by making choices from presented narrative prompts. Each decision, made by tapping on one of 2-4 on-screen options, directly impacts the player's core stats such as Health, Stress, Money, and specific abilities like Strength or Intelligence, which are displayed in a persistent UI. The core loop involves managing these resources through a series of randomly encountered events, where poor choices can lead to negative stat effects or resource consumption, while good choices yield rewards or open new story paths. Progression is achieved through completing specific story arcs, unlocking over 350 unique narrative events across multiple runs, and earning achievements that persist between playthroughs. A run concludes when Health drops to 0 or Stress reaches its maximum of 100, or upon reaching a specific narrative ending, with the objective being to survive and uncover as many story branches as possible.
game_info.description: Navigate the dangerous streets of post-apocalyptic Seoul in 2033. You are a survivor trying to make it through each day by making critical decisions. Manage your Health, Stress, Money, and special abilities (Strength, Intelligence, Charisma) as you encounter various events and characters. Every choice matters - some will help you survive, others may lead to your downfall. Your goal is to survive as many days as possible, accumulate achievements, and unlock new story branches. The game ends when your Health reaches 0, Stress hits 100, or you reach a narrative ending.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys (Up/Down): Navigate between choice options in events
Space: Select the highlighted choice
Z: Quick select first choice (for speed runs)
ENTER: Start game from title screen
ESC: Pause/Unpause game
R: Return to title screen (restart)

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic game mechanics: event progression, choice selection, stat changes, and game flow. Validates that the game can handle continuous play, choices affect stats appropriately, and events cycle correctly.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Make random valid choices from available options at each event. Track stat changes to ensure they update. Continue playing until game over condition is reached naturally through accumulated negative effects.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Game should progress through multiple events without errors. Stats should change based on choices. Eventually reach GAME_OVER state due to Health reaching 0 or Stress reaching 100. No crashes or stuck states.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing optimal survival strategy to achieve maximum days survived. This tests if a skilled player can survive long-term by making smart resource management decisions and balancing multiple stats effectively.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Analyze each event and choice based on current stats. Prioritize choices that maintain Health above 30, keep Stress below 70, and balance resource gains. Avoid high-risk choices when stats are critical. Favor choices that match high player abilities (STR/INT/CHA).
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Survive for at least 20+ days by making optimal choices. Maintain stable stats throughout. Unlock multiple achievements. Demonstrate that skillful play significantly extends survival compared to random choices. Test is successful when achieving a high score.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing edge cases and boundary conditions: minimum/maximum stat values, rapid stat changes, achievement unlocking, and game state persistence across different scenarios.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Deliberately make choices that push stats to extremes. Test healing when health is critical, stress reduction at high stress, spending when money is low. Verify game handles 0 and maximum values correctly.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Game should gracefully handle all stat boundaries. Game over triggers at correct thresholds (Health=0 or Stress=100). Achievements unlock properly. No mathematical errors or undefined behaviors at edge cases.
